name: budgetBreakdown
_note: film-studios.budgetBreakdown is a PURE money-ish calculator registered via registerLensAction (LENS_ACTIONS / domains/filmstudios.js PATH 3, dispatched as handler(ctx, virtualArtifact, input) with input under artifact.data). It splits a totalBudget across 5 fixed-percentage categories (25/40/15/15/5 = 100%). NO wallet, no DB, no CC moved — so this is a CORRECTNESS contract, not a money-mint guard. FAIL-CLOSED (landed 2026-06-27): the prior `parseFloat(data.totalBudget) || 0` passed Infinity / 1e400(→Infinity) straight through (a truthy non-finite that `|| 0` cannot catch), minting non-finite breakdown amounts. The handler now clamps to a finite, non-negative budget before any math (`Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : 0`), so a poisoned numeric clamps to 0 and EVERY output amount stays finite + non-negative + bounded by totalBudget. It returns ok:true (a calculator degrades gracefully, it does not reject) — totalBudget is declared `string` not `number` here precisely because the fail-closed mode is clamp-to-0, not reject, so it is correct (not a V2 ok_true_on_poisoned_number defect). The percentage sum is exactly 100 and the amount sum equals totalBudget by construction.
